text:
Explore (Digging In)

Providing for Individual Needs

As you move around the room listening to your students discuss the problem, take note of the different ways they fill in the rate tables. Students will need to rely on their multiplicative reasoning (proportional reasoning) to make jumps from one quantity to another in the table. Encourage them to fill in what they know first. You can also tell them that they do not need to find the prices in order.

Suggested Questions

  • If you know the price for 10 bars, how can you find the price for 30 bars?
  • If you know the price for 30 bars, how can you find the price for 300?
  • If you know the price for 180 bars, how could you find the price for 90?
